lessonTitle: Recap Module 2: Fundraising Strategies that Drive Results

data.question: Is the following statement true or false? Matching gift arrangements, with the company matching any dollars donated by their employees with an equivalent donation, is an example of corporate partnership.
data.feedback_correct: True! Matching gift arrangements where a company matches employee donations are indeed an example of a corporate partnership.

data.question: Is the following statement true or false? Major gifts are the largest donations an organization receives from a single source in a single fiscal year.
data.feedback_correct: True! Major gifts are defined as the largest donations an organization receives from a single source in a single fiscal year.

data.question: Is the following statement true or false? In Peer-to-Peer fundraising, donors have direct access to friends, family members, coworkers, and other peers that may have never heard of your organization.
data.feedback_correct: True! In Peer-to-Peer fundraising, donors leverage their personal networks — including friends, family, coworkers, and peers — many of whom may never have heard of the organization before.
